description: A first-line oral medicine for type 2 diabetes that lowers blood glucose mainly by reducing the liver's glucose output and improving insulin sensitivity. It is not metabolised and is cleared unchanged by the kidneys.

halfLife.conditions: plasma elimination half-life, healthy subjects (label: "approximately 6.2 hours"); blood half-life is longer (~17.6 h) due to red-cell partitioning — see notes

vd.conditions: apparent volume V/F = 654 (SD 358) L after a single 850 mg oral dose; F folded in and set to 1 (apparent-volume convention)

F.conditions: set to 1 by the apparent-volume convention (V/F used as the volume); true absolute F is 50–60% at 500 mg and is dose-dependent — see notes

tmax.conditions: single 850 mg oral dose, fasting; mean (SD 0.82 h)

fda_label.title: Metformin Hydrochloride Tablet (Granules Pharmaceuticals Inc.) — FDA Structured Product Label, clinical pharmacology

notes: Curated from the FDA Structured Product Label for metformin hydrochloride immediate-release tablets (Granules), single-dose clinical-pharmacology values in healthy subjects. The volume is the APPARENT volume of distribution V/F = 654 (SD 358) L after a single 850 mg dose; F is set to 1 under the apparent-volume convention so the plotted curve reflects C = D/(V/F) = F·D/V (true plasma concentration) without double-counting F. The true absolute oral bioavailability is 50–60% for a 500 mg dose and is DOSE-DEPENDENT (it falls at higher doses). Crucially, the label attributes the lack of dose proportionality to "decreased absorption rather than an alteration in elimination": elimination is first-order and unsaturated, so superposition of repeated IDENTICAL doses (each with the same F and disposition) remains valid — hence linear: true for fixed-dose multiple dosing, even though scaling the dose is not linear. ke is from the plasma elimination half-life (~6.2 h). The label also reports a much longer blood half-life (~17.6 h) because metformin partitions into red blood cells; this tool plots PLASMA concentration, so 6.2 h is the relevant value. Tmax (2.64 h) is taken at the same 850 mg dose as V/F for internal consistency. Metformin is negligibly protein-bound and is cleared renally largely unchanged. Educational illustration only — not for dosing.
